trajectory.description: Starting first iteration: need a baseline model to evaluate the pipeline and measure iteration time. Will use 3D data with a SchNet model from PyG (guaranteed available), train for 5 epochs, then submit eval. This validates the whole workflow and provides a timing baseline.

trajectory.command: python -c "import torch; print('torch', torch.__version__); import torch_geometric; print('pyg', torch_geometric.__version__); from torch_geometric.nn import SchNet; print('SchNet import ok')"
trajectory.description: Observation: train.py exited with code 139 (SIGSEGV) within 6 seconds. Analysis: likely a crash during import of SchNet or PyG library incompatibility. Decision: run a diagnostic to test SchNet import, then adjust model choice or fix import issues. If SchNet import fails, switch to a different model (e.g., PaiNN from torchmdnet).

Observation: train.py crashed with exit code 139 (SIGSEGV) within seconds, no error message. Analysis: Likely a crash during import of SchNet or PyG extensions (torch_scatter) due to incompatible build. Decision: run a diagnostic script to pinpoint the crash, then switch model if needed (e.g., PaiNN or 2D graph).

trajectory.command: python -u -c "import sys; print('start', flush=True); import torch; print('torch ok', flush=True); import torch_geometric; print('pyg ok', flush=True); from torch_geometric.nn import SchNet; print('schnet ok', flush=True); print('success', flush=True)"
trajectory.description: Observation: train.py (and likely any import of SchNet) crashes with SIGSEGV at import time. Exit code 139 indicates segmentation fault. Analysis: The PyG SchNet or its dependency (torch_scatter) has a binary incompatibility with the CUDA runtime or driver in this environment. Since SchNet from torch_geometric.nn is implemented using scatter operations, this is a known issue. TorchMD-Net models (PaiNN, TensorNet) might not suffer from the same import crash. Decision: Before replacing the model, run a diagnostic command to see exactly which import segfaults. This will inform whether we can use another PyG model or must switch to TorchMD-Net entirely.

Observation: All attempts to import SchNet from torch_geometric.nn result in SIGSEGV (exit code 139) during import, even in a simple diagnostic script. Analysis: The crash likely originates from a C extension (torch_scatter or torch_sparse) that is incompatible with the container's CUDA runtime or has a missing symbol. This could affect all torch_geometric operations that rely on scatter ops. TorchMD-Net models also depend on torch_scatter? Need to verify. If scatter is broken, all PyG-based models will fail, and we may need to fall back to a 2D fingerprint model (safe). Decision: Run a comprehensive diagnostic to test imports of torch_geometric basic modules, torch_scatter, torch_cluster, torchmd-net, e3nn, and a minimal PyG Data/DataLoader creation to narrow down the crash. Then select a working approach.

Observation: All previous attempts to import SchNet segfault. We need to find a working 3D model. TorchMD-Net models (PaiNN, ET) may not trigger the scatter crash. Decision: Write a diagnostic script that sequentially tests imports of torch_scatter, torchmdnet, e3nn, and PaiNN creation. Output will show where it crashes. If any succeed, adapt the pipeline to use that model with custom data loading (no PyG Data objects).

trajectory.description: Observation: torch_scatter import crashes with SIGSEGV. All PyG-based models that use scatter will fail. TorchMD-Net also likely depends on scatter. Analysis: 3D graph models are unavailable due to binary incompatibility. Decision: switch to 2D fingerprint approach using RDKit Morgan fingerprints + MLP, which is guaranteed to work without scatter. First, test RDKit import and fingerprint computation in a quick diagnostic. If successful, build full training pipeline with train.py, model.py, predict.py, then submit eval to establish a baseline.

Decision: Update files, run training for 30 epochs, then submit eval next iteration to get baseline MAE and measure iteration time.

trajectory.description: Observation: baseline MAE 0.198 with Morgan-2048 + MLP, training still decreasing. Analysis: additional fingerprint types (MACCS) and feature standardization can improve signal. Longer training (200 epochs) may reduce MAE further. Decision: add MACCS keys (167 bits), standardize features, early stopping (patience=50), train up to 200 epochs and submit eval. Expect MAE ~0.15-0.17.

trajectory.description: Observation: e3nn works without segfault; fingerprint MLP reached MAE 0.163 test. TorchMD-Net no models available, but we can build a custom 3D model using dense matrix operations (no scatter) to utilize coordinate data and potentially achieve much better MAE (~0.03–0.06). Analysis: PCA distance-based invariant model can be implemented with standard PyTorch ops, bypassing the scatter segfault entirely. Decision: write a custom SchNet-like model using padded distance matrices and RBF edges, test on synthetic batch, then train for 5 epochs on full 3D data to validate and submit eval. This should significantly improve MAE if successful.

trajectory.description: Observation: custom 3D SchNet-like model achieved test MAE 0.109 (best so far). Val MAE still decreasing at epoch 50. Analysis: increasing model capacity (hidden_dim, layers, RBF) and longer training with better LR schedule should improve further. Time budget allows ~120 epochs (~54 min). Decision: rewrite model_3d.py with hidden_dim=256, n_rbf=50, 4 layers, residual + LayerNorm, and train_3d.py with cosine annealing + warmup, 120 epochs, submit eval.
